task_completion.description: Agent opens a document editor, notepad, or spreadsheet to create the power audit report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, word processor, or spreadsheet application is open on the screen, intended for recording the power values.

task_completion.description: Agent documents the calculated apparent power value.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The value 16627 (or a value between 16128 and 17126, allowing for 3% tolerance) is written in the document. If using kilo-units, look for ~16.6.

task_completion.description: Agent includes the correct unit for apparent power.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The unit 'VA' or 'kVA' is written next to the apparent power value.

task_completion.description: Agent documents the calculated real (true) power value.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The value 13302 (or a value between 12903 and 13701, allowing for 3% tolerance) is written in the document. If using kilo-units, look for ~13.3.

task_completion.description: Agent includes the correct unit for real (true) power.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The unit 'W' or 'kW' is written next to the real power value.

task_completion.description: Agent documents the calculated reactive power value.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The value 9976 (or a value between 9676 and 10275, allowing for 3% tolerance) is written in the document. If using kilo-units, look for ~9.98.

task_completion.description: Agent includes the correct unit for reactive power.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The unit 'VAR' or 'kVAR' is written next to the reactive power value.

integrity.description: The agent actually calculated the values rather than hallucinating them.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the use of a calculator app, a Python script, terminal math operations, or a web-based calculator to compute the formulas (S = √3 × V × I, etc.).

integrity.description: The final output is written in a genuine document or text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final answers should be typed into a file (e.g., Notepad, TextEdit, Word, or a saved text file in an IDE), not just left in a calculator history or terminal output.

integrity.description: The agent did not cheat by reading the expected answers from the system's metadata or verifier scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no terminal commands like 'cat verifier.py' or 'cat metadata.json' used to extract the exact expected values (16627, 13302, 9976).

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected values (with ±3% tolerance): Apparent Power (S) = 16627 VA (Acceptable: 16128 - 17126 VA). Real Power (P) = 13302 W (Acceptable: 12903 - 13701 W). Reactive Power (Q) = 9976 VAR (Acceptable: 9676 - 10275 VAR). Kilo-unit equivalents (kVA, kW, kVAR) are also perfectly acceptable as long as the decimal is shifted correctly (e.g., 16.6 kVA).
